title: Golden Ticket

headings.text: 2- Steps of a Golden Ticket Attack
b. Forge a Kerberos Ticket-Granting Ticket (TGT)

overview: A Golden Ticket attack abuses the Kerberos authentication protocol by forging Ticket-Granting Tickets (TGTs) using the KRBTGT account’s password hash. In Active Directory, the KRBTGT account is a special account used by the Key Distribution Center (KDC) to encrypt and sign Kerberos tickets. If an attacker compromises the KRBTGT hash, they can mint tickets th

study.whySoc: Use this note as an analyst reference: understand the concept, identify the relevant telemetry, pivot through evidence, and document the decision path clearly.
keyConcepts: 2- Steps of a Golden Ticket Attack
b. Forge a Kerberos Ticket-Granting Ticket (TGT)
workflow: Identify the user, host, process, network flow, or identity object involved.
Collect the highest-value logs first and preserve timestamps and assumptions.
Pivot through related notes, telemetry sources, and MITRE references.
Document findings, gaps, containment options, and detection improvements.
tips: Note: The security identifier (SID) of the account that initiated the logon. If it appears as S-1-0-0, it means “no valid SID” (anonymous or forged session), another strong indicator of manipulation.
Note: If it shows as all zeros ({00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000}), it usually means the session was artificially created (like in a Golden Ticket attack), not from a normal Kerberos process
